question: When does a field receive the touched status?

options.explanation: Changing a value makes the field dirty, not touched
Clicking sets focus, but touched fires when focus is lost
Form submission does not directly update touchedFields

question: What is the difference between dirty and touched?

options.text: dirty — field was interacted with, touched — field value was changed
options.explanation: It is the other way around: dirty means the value was changed, touched means the field lost focus

options.text: dirty — the field value has been changed, touched — the field has lost focus at least once

options.explanation: These are different states: dirty tracks value changes, touched tracks focus loss

options.text: dirty — for the whole form, touched — for individual fields
options.explanation: Both states exist at the field level (dirtyFields, touchedFields) and at the form level (isDirty)

question: What does the reset() method do when called without arguments?

options.text: Clears all fields to empty strings
options.explanation: reset() without arguments resets to defaultValues, not to empty strings

options.text: Resets the form to the values from defaultValues

options.explanation: reset() does not remove fields — it only resets values and states
reset() only affects the form and does not touch the page

question: Which options can be passed to reset() for a partial reset?

options.explanation: keepFocus is not an option of reset() — focus is not managed through reset
